Unfortunately, I am unable to verify any information about the plant "Lonchocarpus seorsus". However, I can provide you with some general information about the Lonchocarpus genus. 

The Lonchocarpus genus is a group of flowering plants in the legume family (Fabaceae), commonly known as "soapbarks" due to their milky sap. These plants have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Research has identified several bioactive constituents in Lonchocarpus species, including alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenes, which are believed to contribute to their health benefits. Traditional herbal preparations made from Lonchocarpus species include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
